qa_pairs.question: What functionalities does the Distributed Debugging Tool (DDT) provide for debugging OpenMP programs?
qa_pairs.answer: For OpenMP programs, DDT offers functionalities similar to its MPI support, including single-click access to threads, the ability to view stacks in parallel, setting thread-specific breakpoints, and comparing expressions across threads.

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations of the Distributed Debugging Tool (DDT) when used for debugging GPU programs, especially mixed MPI/CUDA codes?
qa_pairs.answer: When debugging GPU programs, particularly mixed MPI/CUDA codes, DDT is limited to using one CPU and one GPU per node, and can be utilized across a maximum of 8 nodes.
